event: Our Lady of the Afflicted interceding for suffering people
symbols: Mary enthroned in golden light — the Queen of Heaven interceding for the afflicted
suffering people below — humanity in its brokenness seeking divine aid
Christ Child on Mary's knee — the source of all healing and mercy
monks or saints at the sides — the communion of saints joining in intercession
golden radiance — divine glory and the grace flowing through Mary's mediation
description: A large arched-top altarpiece. In the upper half, the Virgin Mary sits enthroned in golden radiant light, wearing a red dress and blue-grey mantle, with the Christ Child standing on her knee. She extends one hand downward in a gesture of blessing or compassion. Below, a group of afflicted people — including a woman bent in grief, a sick or dying figure lying on the ground, and others reaching upward in supplication. At the sides, monastic or saintly figures kneel in intercession.
theological_description: This altarpiece presents the Virgin Mary as Our Lady of the Afflicted (Consolatrix Afflictorum), interceding before her Son for suffering humanity. The theological basis for Marian intercession rests on the Wedding at Cana (John 2:1-5), where Mary brought human need to Jesus's attention: 'They have no wine.' Her role as compassionate intercessor is rooted in her unique relationship to Christ and her presence at the cross (John 19:25-27), where she shared in the sufferings of her Son. The afflicted people below embody the brokenness described in Matthew 11:28: 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.' The scene visualizes James 5:16: 'The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective' — Mary as the preeminent righteous one whose prayers avail much. The golden light surrounding Mary recalls the woman clothed with the sun of Revelation 12:1, while the suffering below reflects the groaning of creation awaiting redemption (Romans 8:22-23).

scripture_refs.reason: Mary bringing human need to Jesus at Cana — the scriptural basis for her intercessory role
Mary at the cross given as mother to the beloved disciple — her universal maternal care
Come to me all who are weary — the afflicted reaching upward for divine comfort
The woman clothed with the sun — Mary in golden radiance
